description: Comprehensive GUI test suite using DIY computer use (python-xlib). Tests visually and functionally verify all UI overhaul features (US-041–066) via automated screenshot, click, type, and key interactions, with AI-evaluated pass/fail verdicts.

userStories.title: Test Framework — Core Classes and Runner
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the test framework base classes and runner so tests can be defined and executed.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/__init__.py (empty, makes it a package)

Create scripts/gui-tests/framework.py with: TestResult dataclass (name, status, screenshot_paths, criteria, evaluation_notes, duration_seconds), GUITest base class with setUp/tearDown/run methods, TestSuite class that collects and runs GUITest subclasses

GUITest base class provides helper methods: screenshot(name) saves to results/screenshots/ and returns path, wait(seconds) wraps time.sleep, get_window_bounds() returns (x, y, w, h) for the gitslop window
TestSuite.run() iterates tests, catches exceptions per test, collects TestResult list

Create scripts/gui-tests/run.py — entry point that: builds the app (electron-vite build), launches electron --no-sandbox, waits for window, runs TestSuite, kills app, writes JSON report to results/report.json
Runner supports --filter <pattern> to run specific tests by name substring
Console output shows [PASS] / [FAIL] / [ERROR] per test with colors
Exit code 0 if all pass, 1 if any fail
Test with a dummy test that just takes a screenshot and passes
userStories.notes: Foundation. No screen-control.py dependency yet — import python-xlib directly. The app must be built before launching (npx electron-vite build && npx electron --no-sandbox .).

userStories.title: Test Framework — Screen Interaction Helpers
userStories.description: As a test author, I need click, type, key, and drag helpers integrated into the GUITest base class.
acceptanceCriteria: Import and wrap functions from scripts/screen-control.py (or reimplement using python-xlib directly in framework.py)
GUITest gains: click(rel_x, rel_y) clicks at window-relative coordinates, rightclick(rel_x, rel_y), doubleclick(rel_x, rel_y)
GUITest gains: type_text(text) types string, press_key(combo) presses key combo (e.g. 'ctrl+b')
GUITest gains: drag(x1, y1, x2, y2) all window-relative
All click/drag methods automatically translate window-relative to absolute screen coords using get_window_bounds()
GUITest gains: focus_window() raises the gitslop window before interactions
Test with a test that clicks the Settings gear icon and takes a screenshot
userStories.notes: Depends on US-GT-001a for the framework. The screen-control.py already has all the python-xlib logic — import it as a module or copy the relevant functions.

userStories.title: Test Framework — Window Resize and Multi-Size Support
userStories.description: As a test author, I need to resize the GitSlop window for responsive layout testing.
acceptanceCriteria: GUITest gains: resize_window(width, height) resizes the gitslop window via X11 ConfigureWindow
GUITest gains: reset_window_size() restores to default 1280x800
Runner supports --size WxH flag to set initial window size
Verify resize works: resize to 800x500, screenshot shows smaller window, resize back to 1280x800
Window position is re-read after resize (bounds may change)
userStories.notes: Depends on US-GT-001b. Use Xlib.X.ConfigureWindow with width/height fields. The window manager may add decorations — measure actual content area.

userStories.title: AI Screenshot Evaluator
userStories.description: As a developer, I need screenshots saved with criteria metadata so they can be AI-evaluated for pass/fail.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/evaluator.py with evaluate(screenshot_path, criteria_text) function
For now, evaluate() returns verdict='needs_review' and saves criteria alongside screenshot in report
Each screenshot in the JSON report includes: path, criteria, verdict, notes
GUITest gains: assert_visual(name, criteria) — takes screenshot, calls evaluator, stores result
Baseline screenshots saved to scripts/gui-tests/baselines/{test_name}/ on first run
Report JSON is structured for easy programmatic parsing by Claude
userStories.notes: The real AI evaluation happens when Claude reads screenshots during a session. This just structures the data. Baseline saving enables future pixel-diff regression testing.

userStories.title: Test Repo Fixtures
userStories.description: As a test author, I need a deterministic test repo with known branches, commits, tags, and working tree state.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/fixtures.py with create_test_repo() and cleanup_test_repo(path)

create_test_repo() creates a temp directory with: git init, 5 commits on main with known messages and file contents, a 'feature' branch with 3 commits, merged back to main (merge commit), 2 tags ('v1.0', 'v2.0'), 1 stash, one staged file, one unstaged modified file, one untracked file
All commit messages, author names, dates are deterministic (use GIT_AUTHOR_DATE and GIT_COMMITTER_DATE env vars)
cleanup_test_repo() removes the temp directory
Add open_test_repo(repo_path) helper that clicks 'Open Repository' in toolbar/welcome screen and navigates to the path
Test with a test that creates the repo, verifies files exist, cleans up
userStories.notes: Use subprocess.run for git commands. Set GIT_AUTHOR_NAME, GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL, GIT_AUTHOR_DATE, GIT_COMMITTER_DATE for determinism. The open_repo flow may need to interact with the native file dialog — consider if electron supports a command-line arg to open a repo path directly.

userStories.title: npm Integration and Directory Setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the test suite callable via npm run gui-test with proper directory structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'gui-test' script to package.json: 'python3 scripts/gui-tests/run.py'
Create scripts/gui-tests/results/.gitkeep (gitignored except .gitkeep)
Create scripts/gui-tests/baselines/.gitkeep
Add scripts/gui-tests/results/ to .gitignore (except .gitkeep)
Add scripts/gui-tests/tests/__init__.py (test case package)
Verify npm run gui-test launches the runner and produces a report
userStories.notes: Depends on US-GT-001a. Keep baselines in git (they're reference screenshots). Results are ephemeral and gitignored.

userStories.title: Test — Welcome Screen
userStories.description: As a tester, I want to verify the welcome screen renders correctly with all expected elements.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/tests/test_welcome.py with class TestWelcomeScreen(GUITest)

Test takes screenshot on launch, asserts visual criteria: 'GS wordmark, GitSlop title, subtitle, three action cards (Open/Clone/Init) with Lucide icons, keyboard shortcut hints, Recent Repositories section, no emoji'
Test verifies toolbar shows only Open, Clone, Init (no Pull/Push/Fetch)
Test verifies status bar shows 'No repository open'
Test toggles theme (clicks Sun/Moon icon in titlebar), takes screenshot of light theme, toggles back
All screenshots saved with descriptive names (welcome_dark.png, welcome_light.png)
userStories.notes: This test runs on app launch before any repo is opened. The welcome screen is the default state.

userStories.title: Test — Toolbar and Titlebar
userStories.description: As a tester, I want to verify toolbar context-aware buttons and titlebar layout after opening a repo.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/tests/test_toolbar_titlebar.py
Test opens the gitslop repo (click recent repo or use fixture), takes screenshot
Asserts toolbar shows: Pull, Push, Fetch, Branch, Merge, Stash — all with Lucide icons, no emoji
Asserts Settings gear always visible on far right
Asserts titlebar shows: GitSlop wordmark (left), repo name + branch (center), window controls (right)
Test clicks Fetch button, takes screenshot showing inline spinner during operation
Test clicks center repo info in titlebar, takes screenshot showing 'Copied!' feedback
userStories.notes: Depends on US-GT-003 for opening a repo. The fetch operation needs a remote — the gitslop repo has origin configured.

userStories.title: Test — Status Bar
userStories.description: As a tester, I want to verify the status bar information and interactions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/tests/test_statusbar.py
With repo open: asserts branch name with GitBranch icon visible in left section
Asserts right section shows: UTF-8, LF indicators, refresh button, notification bell
Hovers over status bar, takes screenshot — text should become more visible on hover
Clicks notification bell, takes screenshot of history dropdown
Presses Escape to close dropdown, verifies it closes
userStories.notes: Status bar is 24px tall at the bottom. Coordinates will be near window_height - 12 for clicking elements.

userStories.title: Test — Sidebar Expanded and Sections
userStories.description: As a tester, I want to verify the sidebar renders with all sections and is scrollable.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/tests/test_sidebar.py
Ensures sidebar is expanded (press Ctrl+B if collapsed), takes screenshot
Asserts: sidebar visible on left with Branches/Files tabs, collapse button at top
Asserts: Branches tab shows local branches with GitBranch icons, current branch highlighted
Asserts: all icons are Lucide (no emoji/unicode)
Clicks a section header chevron to collapse/expand a section, takes screenshot of both states
userStories.notes: Sidebar may start collapsed if previous session left it that way. Always ensure expanded state first.

userStories.title: Test — Sidebar Icon Rail Collapse
userStories.description: As a tester, I want to verify sidebar collapse to icon rail and floating overlay.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/tests/test_sidebar_collapse.py
Clicks collapse button (PanelLeftClose icon at top of sidebar)
Takes screenshot — asserts 48px icon rail visible with section icons
Clicks a section icon in the rail — takes screenshot of floating overlay panel
Clicks outside overlay — verifies it closes
Clicks expand button (PanelLeftOpen) — takes screenshot of restored full sidebar
Restores sidebar to expanded state at end of test
userStories.notes: The collapse button is at the top-left of the sidebar. The icon rail is 48px wide.

userStories.title: Test — Three-Column Layout and Panel Dividers
userStories.description: As a tester, I want to verify the three-column layout, panel resizing, and terminal toggle.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/tests/test_layout.py
With repo open and no commit selected: takes screenshot, asserts two columns (sidebar + center)
Clicks a commit row in the graph — takes screenshot showing third column (detail panel) appeared
Drags a panel divider to resize, takes before/after screenshots showing size changed
Presses Ctrl+` to toggle terminal, takes screenshot showing terminal panel at bottom
Presses Ctrl+` again to hide terminal
Closes detail panel (clicks X), verifies it disappears
userStories.notes: Resize handle position depends on sidebar width. Use drag() with approximate divider coordinates.

userStories.title: Test — Hybrid Detail Panel (Inline vs Overlay)
userStories.description: As a tester, I want to verify the detail panel switches between inline column and overlay at different widths.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/tests/test_detail_panel_modes.py
At 1280x800: clicks a commit, takes screenshot — detail panel is inline third column
Resizes window to 1100x700: takes screenshot — detail panel should now be overlay with backdrop shadow
Presses Escape — overlay dismisses
Resizes back to 1280x800, restores default state
userStories.notes: Depends on US-GT-001c for resize_window(). The DETAIL_PANEL_BREAKPOINT is 1400px, so even 1280 should be overlay mode. May need to test at 1500px for inline mode.

userStories.title: Test — SVG Commit Graph Rendering
userStories.description: As a tester, I want to verify the commit graph renders with SVG nodes, lanes, and labels.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/tests/test_commit_graph.py
Takes screenshot of commit graph area with repo open
Asserts visual criteria: colored circle nodes, vertical branch lane lines, HEAD commit with glow effect, branch/tag labels as pill badges
Asserts each commit row shows: short hash, message, author, relative date
Asserts 'ralph/ui-overhaul' (or current branch) label visible on HEAD commit
Scrolls down in commit graph (multiple mouse wheel events), takes screenshot — no gaps or missing nodes
userStories.notes: The commit graph uses react-window for virtualization. Scrolling is via the graph's internal scroll container.

userStories.title: Test — Commit Graph Interaction (Click, Context Menu, Keyboard)
userStories.description: As a tester, I want to verify commit selection, context menus, and keyboard navigation in the graph.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/tests/test_commit_interaction.py
Clicks a commit row — takes screenshot, asserts it highlights with accent color and detail panel opens
Right-clicks a commit — takes screenshot of context menu with items: checkout, cherry-pick, revert, reset, create branch, create tag, copy SHA
Presses Escape to close context menu
Presses Down arrow 3 times then Enter — verifies selection moved and detail panel updated
Hovers over a commit for 800ms — takes screenshot showing tooltip with commit message
userStories.notes: Context menu position depends on where the commit row is. Use coordinates from the commit row area (roughly x=400, y varies by row).

userStories.title: Test — Commit Detail Panel Content
userStories.description: As a tester, I want to verify the detail panel shows full commit info, file list, and copy functionality.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/tests/test_detail_panel.py
Clicks a commit, takes screenshot of detail panel
Asserts: subject bold, full SHA visible, author name + email, absolute and relative date
Asserts: Changed Files section lists files with Lucide status icons (FilePlus/FileMinus/FileEdit)
Asserts: file count summary with +insertions -deletions
Clicks the SHA copy button — takes screenshot showing copy feedback
Clicks close (X) — panel dismisses
userStories.notes: The detail panel is on the right side. SHA copy button is near the top of the panel.

userStories.title: Test — Diff Viewer in Detail Panel
userStories.description: As a tester, I want to verify inline and side-by-side diff views in the commit detail panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/tests/test_diff_viewer.py
Clicks a commit, then clicks a file in the Changed Files list
Takes screenshot — asserts diff renders with green additions and red deletions
Asserts diff header shows file path and change type
Clicks 'Split' toggle button — takes screenshot of side-by-side view
Clicks next file navigation button (or presses ]) — takes screenshot showing different file's diff
Clicks back to Inline mode
userStories.notes: Choose a commit with multiple changed files for this test. The gitslop repo has plenty of multi-file commits.

userStories.title: Test — Staging Area Layout and Stage/Unstage
userStories.description: As a tester, I want to verify the staging area two-column layout, stage, and unstage operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/tests/test_staging.py
Opens test repo (with staged/unstaged/untracked files) or uses gitslop repo with pending changes
Takes screenshot — asserts: staging area below graph, collapsible header with file count badge, two-column layout (Unstaged left, Staged right)
Asserts: each file shows Lucide status icon, filename, directory path (muted)
Clicks + button on an unstaged file — takes screenshot, file moved to staged column
Clicks - button on the staged file — takes screenshot, file returned to unstaged
Clicks 'Stage All' — verifies all files move to staged column
Clicks 'Unstage All' — verifies all return to unstaged
userStories.notes: The staging area needs files with changes. Use the test repo fixture which has staged/unstaged/untracked files ready.

userStories.title: Test — Context Menus Everywhere
userStories.description: As a tester, I want to verify styled context menus appear on commits, branches, files, and tags.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/tests/test_context_menus.py
Right-clicks a commit row — takes screenshot, asserts styled context menu with Lucide icons and labels
Presses Escape to close
Right-clicks a branch in sidebar — takes screenshot of branch context menu
Presses Escape to close
Right-clicks a file in staging area — takes screenshot of file context menu
Asserts: dangerous items (delete) styled in red, menu stays within viewport
userStories.notes: Must have sidebar expanded to right-click branches. Staging area must have files to right-click.

userStories.title: Test — Responsive Layout at Multiple Sizes
userStories.description: As a tester, I want to verify layout adapts at 1280x800, 1024x768, 900x600, and 800x500.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/tests/test_responsive.py
At 1280x800: takes screenshot — asserts full layout, toolbar labels visible, sidebar expanded
Resizes to 1024x768: takes screenshot — asserts sidebar still visible (may be expanded or collapsed depending on threshold)
Resizes to 900x600: takes screenshot — asserts sidebar auto-collapsed to icon rail
Resizes to 800x500: takes screenshot — asserts toolbar icon-only (no labels), status bar center hidden, titlebar repo path hidden
Resizes back to 1280x800: takes screenshot — asserts sidebar restored, labels returned
Asserts text truncates with ellipsis at narrow sizes (no wrapping/overflow)
userStories.notes: Depends on US-GT-001c for resize_window(). Each resize needs a small delay (300ms) for responsive effects and animations.

userStories.title: Test — Keyboard Shortcuts
userStories.description: As a tester, I want to verify all major keyboard shortcuts trigger correct actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/tests/test_keyboard.py
Presses Ctrl+B: takes before/after screenshots — sidebar toggles (expanded to collapsed or vice versa)
Presses Ctrl+B again to restore
Presses Ctrl+`: takes screenshot — terminal panel toggles on
Presses Ctrl+` again to hide terminal
Presses Ctrl+K: takes screenshot — search/command palette opens
Presses Escape to close palette
Presses Ctrl+Shift+F: takes screenshot — fetch operation starts (spinner visible in toolbar)
userStories.notes: press_key helper must handle combo keys (ctrl+b, ctrl+shift+f). Needs repo open for git shortcuts.

userStories.title: Test — Notification System
userStories.description: As a tester, I want to verify toast notifications and notification history.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/tests/test_notifications.py
Triggers a notification by clicking Fetch (success toast appears)
Takes screenshot within 2s — asserts toast visible in bottom-right with Lucide icon and message
Waits 5s, takes screenshot — toast should have auto-dismissed
Clicks notification bell in status bar — takes screenshot of history dropdown with past notification
Presses Escape to close dropdown
userStories.notes: Toast timing: 4s for success, 8s for errors. Take screenshot quickly after triggering.

userStories.title: Test — Both Themes
userStories.description: As a tester, I want to verify dark and light themes render correctly across all components.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/tests/test_themes.py
Takes screenshot in dark theme (default) with repo open — asserts dark background, light text
Clicks theme toggle (Sun/Moon icon in titlebar top-right area)
Takes screenshot in light theme — asserts light background, dark text, all components visible
Takes screenshot of commit graph in light theme — asserts nodes and lines visible
Toggles back to dark theme, takes confirmation screenshot
userStories.notes: Theme toggle is a button near the window controls in the titlebar (right side). Look for Sun or Moon icon.

userStories.title: Test — Multi-Repo Tabs
userStories.description: As a tester, I want to verify tab bar appears with multiple repos and tab switching works.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/tests/test_tabs.py
Opens the gitslop repo (from recent), takes screenshot — no tab bar visible (single repo)
Creates a temp test repo via fixtures, opens it via Open Repository
Takes screenshot — tab bar should appear with two tabs
Asserts active tab visually distinct (highlighted/underlined)
Clicks the first tab — verifies view switches to gitslop repo
Closes a tab (clicks X) — verifies tab removed
Cleans up test repo
userStories.notes: Opening a second repo may involve the native file dialog. Consider if Init can create an in-place temp repo to avoid dialog interaction.

userStories.title: Test — Git Operations End-to-End
userStories.description: As a tester, I want to verify actual git operations (stage, commit, stash, branch) work through the UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/tests/test_git_ops.py
Creates test repo with fixtures, opens it in GitSlop
Modifies a file in the test repo via Python (write to a file), waits for watcher to detect change
Takes screenshot — asserts new file appears in Unstaged Changes
Clicks + to stage the file, takes screenshot — file in Staged column
Types commit message in commit form, clicks Commit, takes screenshot — new commit visible in graph
Opens stash dialog (Ctrl+Shift+S or click Stash), stashes changes, takes screenshot — stash in sidebar
Cleans up test repo
userStories.notes: This is the most complex test — it modifies real git state. Must use the test repo (not gitslop repo) to avoid polluting the project. Cleanup is critical.

userStories.title: Test — Loading States and Skeletons
userStories.description: As a tester, I want to verify skeleton screens appear during loading instead of blank screens.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/tests/test_loading.py
Opens a repo and rapidly takes screenshots (every 100ms for 2 seconds) during initial load
Reviews rapid screenshots — asserts at least one shows skeleton placeholders (gray pulsing rectangles)
Asserts no screenshot shows a completely blank/white center panel
Saves the sequence as loading_001.png through loading_020.png for review
userStories.notes: Skeleton screens are transient — they appear for a fraction of a second during load. Rapid screenshot capture is needed. This test may be flaky on fast machines where loading is instant.

userStories.title: Test — Panel Animations
userStories.description: As a tester, I want to verify smooth panel animations exist (no layout flash).
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/gui-tests/tests/test_animations.py
Collapses sidebar and captures 5 rapid screenshots during the 200ms animation
Asserts at least one screenshot shows intermediate state (partially collapsed, not just before/after)
Expands sidebar, captures rapid screenshots — same assertion
Opens terminal (Ctrl+`), captures rapid screenshots during animation
Asserts no screenshot shows a blank flash or layout jitter
userStories.notes: Animations are 200ms. Take screenshots every 50ms for ~400ms to capture the transition. This is a polish verification test.
